question.title: PR-A4 multi-step driver + diff/patch engine + CI gate runner — implementation plan v1 adversarial review (iter 1)
question.body:
PR-A4 (FAZ-A Tranche A 5/6) plan v1: 8 new source modules + 6 test files, scope ≈2880 LOC. Builds on PR-A3 single-step `Executor.run_step` primitive to add (a) `MultiStepDriver` looping over `workflow_definition.steps` with on_failure handling, HITL/approval resume, per-step CAS; (b) `ao_kernel/patch/` new package: `preview_diff` + `apply_patch --3way` + deterministic reverse-diff rollback, fail-closed pre-flight check, binary-diff rejection; (c) `ao_kernel/ci/` new package: `run_pytest` + `run_ruff` subprocess orchestration inside PR-A3 SandboxedEnvironment, fail-closed (flaky tolerance = 0).

9 spec-level questions (plan §17):
Q1 Multi-step loop resume semantics: ardışık happy path vs. per-step re-entry; driver crash + restart idempotent resume via completed_steps list vs. explicit current_step_index
Q2 retry_once state transition atomicity: single CAS vs. two-phase; retry_counter persistence across crashes
Q3 Patch 3-way conflict UX: failed vs. waiting_approval when `.rej` files present; step.on_failure schema constraint
Q4 Cross-ref double-check: PR-A3 invariant (Executor per-step) + driver start-of-workflow; double-check acceptable vs. driver-only + executor cache
Q5 CI subprocess hermeticity: pytest sys.path/PYTHONPATH resolution (pip install -e . vs. PYTHONPATH env vs. --rootdir); ruff invocation form (python3 -m ruff vs. basename allowlist)
Q6 CI timeout semantics: CIResult.status='timeout' return vs. CITimeoutError raise
Q7 Budget allocation: multi-step common pool vs. per-step quota; patch/CI time_seconds on common axis vs. subprocess_seconds separate
Q8 Step dispatch model: workflow-definition.schema.v1 has step.actor {adapter,ao-kernel,human,system} + step.gate but NO step.kind enum. Three options for ao-kernel/system step operation resolution: (A) step_name convention, (B) additive schema patch with step.operation enum, (C) reuse required_capabilities with internal '_' prefix. Which is fail-closed + explicit + backward-compatible?
Q9 PR-A4 split recommendation: ~2880 LOC + 92 tests in single PR (PR-A3 pattern) vs. A4a (patch/+ci/ primitives) + A4b (multi_step_driver + integration)

Bozulmaz ilkeler (PR-A3'ten devralınan + PR-A4 yeni): POSIX-only, inherit_from_parent=False strict, PATH anchoring realpath+prefix, per-run events.jsonl.lock + monotonic seq, cross-ref per-call, canonical event order, JSONPath $.key(.key)* subset, text/plain fallback triple gate, patch pre-flight --check, reverse diff deterministic path + atomic write, rollback idempotent, CI flaky tolerance=0, retry_once CAS semantics (running→running + retry_counter bump), multi-step state CAS per-step, HITL resume idempotent payload, escalate_to_human transition to waiting_approval. Core dep unchanged (jsonschema>=4.23.0). No new schema (pending Q8 resolution — Option B may require workflow-definition.schema.v1.1 minor bump).

Context refs: .claude/plans/PR-A4-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md (v1, ~680 LOC), .claude/plans/PR-A3-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md (pattern ref, v2 shipped), ao_kernel/executor/{executor,policy_enforcer,evidence_emitter,adapter_invoker,worktree_builder}.py (PR-A3 shipped), ao_kernel/workflow/{state_machine,run_store,primitives,budget,registry}.py (PR-A1/A2 shipped), ao_kernel/adapters/manifest_loader.py (PR-A2 shipped), ao_kernel/defaults/schemas/{workflow-definition,workflow-run,agent-adapter-contract}.schema.v1.json, ao_kernel/defaults/policies/policy_worktree_profile.v1.json, docs/{ADAPTERS,WORKTREE-PROFILE,EVIDENCE-TIMELINE,DEMO-SCRIPT}.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/plans/TRANCHE-STRATEGY-V2.md.

Expected: PARTIAL iter-1 with N blocking + M warning, 2-iter convergence pattern established across CNS-019/020/021/022. Per CLAUDE.md §15 rule, open via MCP transport (mcp__codex__codex new thread) instead of codex exec.
